title: torilis helvetica var. neglecta
intro: Torilis helvetica var. neglecta, also known as wild chervil or horned poppy, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small, white or yellowish flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as a digestive aid, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of Torilis helvetica var. neglecta include its potential to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be dried and used as an herbal supplement.
